english: 'sprós - pa - čan (spros pa can), busy, employed, occupied; sprós-bčas (spros bcas), id.; čós-kyi (chos kyi) and °jig - rtén - gyi ('jig rten gyi) sprós - pa (spros pa), spiritual and secular business'; Sch.: 'sprós kun (spros kun), all affairs'; I met only with sprós-pa méd-pa (spros pa med pa) or čód-pa (chod pa), or sprós-brál (spros bral), denoting the state of an absolute inactivity, such as belongs to Buddha in the state of čos-sku (chos sku), (v. sku (sku) 2) Pth., Mil.
1. the letter p', aspirate, the English p in pass. - 2. num. figure: 14.

I. vulgo ཨ་ཕ་, ཨ་པུ་, ă-p'a (a pha), ă-pu (a pu), (Cs. also ཨ་ཏ་ ă-ta (a ta)) 1. father, resp. yab (yab) (yet also p'a (pha) is used, e.g. when Milaraspa is addressed by his female disciples, as well as in prayers to defunct saints Mil.) - 2. a male, not castrated, animal (vulg. likewise ă-p'a (a pha)). Comp. p'a-glaṅ (pha glang) bull. - p'a-rjés-bú (pha rjes bu), Sch., a child born after its father's death. - p'a-rtá (pha rta), stallion. - *p'a-nór* (*pha nor*), patrimony C., W. - p'a-spád (pha spad) (Sch. also p'ád (phad)) v. spád (spad); p'a-spún (pha spun) v. spún (spun). - p'a-p'ág (pha phag), boar. - p'a-má (pha ma), parents, p'a-má-la gús-pa (pha ma la gus pa), Stg.; *p'a-ma-méd-k'an* (*pha ma med mkhan*), W., orphan; also father or mother, parent; p'a-má-gčig-pa (pha ma gcig pa), brothers and sisters born of the same parents. - p'a-miṅ (pha ming), relations on the father's side; btsún-moi p'a-miṅ bós-so (btsun mo'i pha ming bos so), Glr., he invited the relations of his wife's father; p'a (daṅ) més (-po) (pha (dang) mes (-po)), ancestors; p'a-més śí-bai dón-du (pha mes shi ba'i don du), for the (defunct) ancestors, Wdn.; - p'a-tsáb (pha tshab) 1. foster-father, guardian, Sch. 2. father to a country (?). - p'a-tsán (pha tshan), Mil. 1. cousin by the father's side (patruelis) C. 2. also = p'a-spún (pha spun) (?). - p'a-gźi (pha gzhi) = *p'a-nór* (*pha nor*), C. - p'a-yáṅ (pha yang), Sch., step-father; - p'a-yúl (pha yul), fatherland, native country, frq.; p'a-yúl-la čágs-pa (pha yul la chags pa) or srég-pa (sreg pa), love of country. - p'a-g.yag (pha g.yag), yak-bull. - p'a-g.yar (pha g.yar), step-father, foster-father, Cs. - p'a-rá (pha ra), he-goat, buck. II. root for the terms: beyond, onward, farther on; p'a-ga (pha ga), the opposite side; ču p'a-yar t'ón-nas (chu pha yar thon nas), to get to the opposite bank or shore, Mil. (not frq.). - p'a-gi (pha gi), 1. that which is on the other side, Sch. 2. C., also Pth., Mil.: yonder; p'a-gíi ri de (pha gi'i ri de), that mountain yonder, Pth. 3. col.: he. - p'a-gir (pha gir), there, thither. - p'a-ṅós (pha ngos) = p'a-ról (pha rol), p'a-ról-tu (pha rol tu) Lh. - p'a-mt'a (pha mtha'), the other end, the other boundary, Cs.; p'a-mt'a-méd (pha mtha' med), without boundary, endless, Cs. - p'a-p'yogs (pha phyogs) C. = p'a-ga (pha ga). - p'a-tsád (pha tshad), p'a-zád (pha zad), distance; p'a-tsád číg-na (pha tshad cig na), at a small distance (from the town), Pth.; dé-nas p'a-zád číg-na (de nas pha zad cig na), a bit farther on, Dzl.; p'a-tsád číg-tu t'ón-nas (pha tshad cig tu thon nas), stepping a little aside, Pth.; p'a-zád °gró-ba (pha zad 'gro ba), to go on, Dzl. frq. - p'a-rí (pha ri) the mountain on the other side. - p'a-ról (pha rol), in B. very frq. 1. the other side; opposite side, counterparty. 2. for p'a-ról-pa (pha rol pa), -na (-na), -tu (-tu) v. below; p'a-ról-tu (pha rol tu), over to the other side, skyél-ba (skyel ba), to carry, p'yín-pa (phyin pa), to get to the other side, esp. in reference to the Mahāyāna doctrine of crossing the stream of time to the shore of rest, of Nirwāṇa; gen. as sbst. = पारमिता, means of crossing (Was. perfections, Köpp. cardinal-virtues); gen. six of them are reckoned: sbyín-pa (sbyin pa), ts'úl-k'rims (tshul khrims), bzód-pa (bzod pa), brtsón-°grús (brtson 'grus), bsám-gtán (bsam gtan), śés-ráb (shes rab); sometimes only five, at other times even ten, by adding t'ábs (thabs), smón-lam (smon lam), stóbs (stobs), yé-śes (ye shes); sbyín-pai (sbyin pa'i), śés-ráb-kyi p'a-ról-tu p'yín-pa (shes rab kyi pha rol tu phyin pa), to have stepped over or crossed by means of beneficence, wisdom etc. (or more naturally: to have got to the end of beneficence etc., to have fully achieved, accomplished it; sbst. the full accomplishment of etc.). - p'a-ról-na (pha rol na), adv., on the other side; postp. e. gen. beyond, behind, with regard to space, Sambh.; extending
